steps.title: Find angle Y in the sub-triangle
steps.content:
Point $W$ on hypotenuse $YZ$ creates right triangle $XWY$ with a right angle at $W$ (since $WX \perp YZ$). In this right triangle, the angle at $Y$ is the same as $\angle XYZ$ in the original triangle. Therefore:
$$\tan(\angle Y) = \frac{\text{opposite}}{\text{adjacent}} = \frac{WX}{WY} = \frac{429}{572}$$

Both values share a common factor: $429 = 3 \times 143$ and $572 = 4 \times 143$. So:
$$\tan(\angle Y) = \frac{3 \times 143}{4 \times 143} = \frac{3}{4}$$

steps.title: Use the complementary angle relationship
steps.content: In triangle $XYZ$, angle $X = 90°$, so the other two angles sum to \$90°$: $$\angle Y + \angle Z = 90°$$ This means $\angle Z = 90° - \angle Y$, so: $$\tan(\angle Z) = \tan(90° - \angle Y) = \cot(\angle Y) = \frac{1}{\tan(\angle Y)}$$ Substituting $\tan(\angle Y) = \frac{3}{4}$: $$\tan(\angle Z) = \frac{1}{\frac{3}{4}} = \frac{4}{3}$$ That matches choice D — D is correct.
